sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: Mainz 05 has shown mixed form in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, though they managed a strong 4-1 win recently. Leverkusen, despite being higher in the standings, has struggled lately with only 1 win, 3 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5 matches, indicating vulnerability.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Leverkusen has dominated recent head-to-head encounters, winning 4 of the last 5 against Mainz 05, including the most recent 1-0 victory. Mainz has failed to keep a clean sheet against Leverkusen in 15 consecutive matches, suggesting defensive struggles.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen boasts a stronger attack with 70 goals scored (2nd in the league) compared to Mainz’s 53 (8th), and their league position (2nd vs 6th) reflects better overall consistency. Mainz, however, has a solid defense (3rd fewest goals conceded), which could keep the game close.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Mainz relies on Jonathan Burkardt (17 goals) for attacking threat, while Leverkusen’s Patrik Schick (19 goals) and Florian Wirtz (12 assists) pose significant danger. Both teams have minor absences, but Leverkusen’s squad depth should help them cope better.

sections.subtitle: Match Context and Trends
sections.text: Head-to-head trends point to high-scoring games (over 2.5 goals in 5 of last 6 meetings) and Leverkusen often scoring first. Mainz’s recent defensive lapses and Leverkusen’s attacking prowess tilt the balance slightly towards the away side.
